data.prompt_text: Two hikers at points B and C are 359 feet apart on a flat plain. They are looking at a landmark at A. They measure \angle B = 38.4^\circ and \angle C = 36.6^\circ. What is the distance from hiker B to the landmark (distance AB)?
data.solution_steps:
\text{We are given side } a = 359 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 38.4^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 36.6^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 38.4^\circ - 36.6^\circ = 105.0^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(36.6^\circ)} = \frac{359}{\sin(105.0^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{359\sin(36.6^\circ)}{\sin(105.0^\circ)}

c \approx 221.6

\text{We are given side } a = 476 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 21.1^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 83.2^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 21.1^\circ - 83.2^\circ = 75.7^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(83.2^\circ)} = \frac{476}{\sin(75.7^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{476\sin(83.2^\circ)}{\sin(75.7^\circ)}

c \approx 487.8

\text{We are given side } a = 452 \text{, and angles } \angle B = 73.3^\circ \text{ and } \angle C = 45.0^\circ.

\text{We need to find the distance } AB \text{, which corresponds to side } c.

\text{Step 1: Find the missing angle } A.

\angle A = 180^\circ - \angle B - \angle C

\angle A = 180^\circ - 73.3^\circ - 45.0^\circ = 61.7^\circ

\text{Step 2: Set up the Law of Sines to solve for } c.

\frac{c}{\sin(C)} = \frac{a}{\sin(A)}

\frac{c}{\sin(45.0^\circ)} = \frac{452}{\sin(61.7^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{452\sin(45.0^\circ)}{\sin(61.7^\circ)}

c \approx 363.0

\frac{c}{\sin(34.2^\circ)} = \frac{214}{\sin(31.3^\circ)}

\text{Step 3: Isolate } c \text{ and evaluate.}

c = \frac{214\sin(34.2^\circ)}{\sin(31.3^\circ)}

c \approx 231.5
